title: Sri Lankan woman held for obtaining Indian passport with forged documents
byline: A 28-year-old Sri Lankan woman was arrested at Chennai International Airport for obtaining an Indian passport with forged documents and attempting to travel abroad.

title: Iraqi militia claims drone attack targeting US troops in Baghdad
byline: An Iraqi Shiite militia claimed a drone attack Monday targeting U.S. troops at the airport in Iraq’s capital, Baghdad, further widening the retaliation over the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: US, Arab nations slam Tehran’s drone, missiles strikes after US-Israel joint military action on Iran
byline: The United States and six Arab nations on Monday issued a strongly worded statement condemning Iran’s recent missile and drone attacks across West Asia, calling them ‘indiscriminate’, ‘reckless’, and a dangerous escalation that threatens regional stability.

title: Trump says combat operations in Iran will continue until all objectives achieved
byline: U.S. President Donald Trump warned on Sunday that combat operations in Iran were continuing and would carry on until all of Washington’s objectives are achieved.

title: GMOA withdraws from ‘Arogya’ Project, cites lack of transparency and political motives
byline: The Government Medical Officers’ Association (GMOA) has announced that it will withdraw from the “Arogya” project launched by the Ministry of Health with effect from today (02).

title: Israel strikes Beirut’s southern suburbs after Hezbollah attacks
byline: Israel carried out heavy airstrikes on the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut on Monday, after the Iran-backed group launched missiles and drones towards Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

title: Govt launches special programme to safeguard tourists and migrant workers amid Middle East crisis
byline: In view of the unrest that has arisen in the Middle East and the Gulf region, the Government has launched a series of special operations to look into the safety and welfare of foreign tourists currently in Sri Lanka, as well as to ensure the protection and well-being of Sri Lankan migrant workers overseas.

title: Youth who attended music concert at Colombo Port City found dead
byline: A male body has been discovered while floating in the sea near the Colombo Port City, police said.

title: Iran internet blackout continues with no end in sight
byline: For more than 36 hours, internet access has been largely blocked in Iran after US and Israeli strikes on the country, according to internet monitoring organisations.

title: UK’s Starmer says US can use British bases for defensive strikes against Iran missiles
byline: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Sunday that his country has accepted a U.S. request to use British bases for defensive strikes against Iranian missiles in storage depots or launchers.

title: War photographer Paul Conroy dies as tributes paid
byline: Tributes have been paid to the war photographer Paul Conroy who has died at the age of 61.

title: Israel hits Tehran again after killing Khamenei, leadership council takes over
byline: Israel launched a new wave of strikes on Tehran on Sunday and Iran responded with more missile barrages, a day after the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei pitched the Middle East and the global economy into deepening uncertainty.

title: Gulf states say they reserve right to respond to Iranian ‘aggression’
byline: Gulf states vowed to defend themselves against Iranian attacks, including by “responding to the aggression” if need be, after the Gulf Cooperation Council convened via video-link on Sunday to formulate a unified response.

title: Mainly dry weather expected in most parts of the island
byline: Mainly dry weather will prevail in the most parts of the island, today (02), the Department of Meteorology said.

title: War in Middle East – Latest
byline: Urging Iranians to overthrow their clerical rulers, the US launched “major combat operations” along with Israel against Iran early on Saturday.

title: OPEC+ debates oil output boost as war disrupts shipments
byline: OPEC+ will consider a larger-than-expected oil output increase on Sunday, two OPEC+ sources said after the U.S.-Israeli war on OPEC+ member Iran and Tehran’s retaliation led to shipment disruptions in the Middle East.

title: All SriLankan Airlines flights to Middle East cancelled today
byline: SriLankan Airlines has announced that all flights to the Middle East scheduled for today (01) have been cancelled in response to the ongoing situation in the region.

title: Oil and gas majors and traders suspend shipments via Hormuz, sources say
byline: Several tanker owners, oil majors and trading houses have suspended crude oil, fuel and liquefied natural gas shipments via the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran and Tehran said it had closed navigation, trading sources said on Saturday.

title: At least 6 killed as Shiites storm US Consulate in Pakistan over killing of Iran’s supreme leader
byline: At least six people were killed and about a dozen were wounded in violent clashes with police and paramilitary forces Sunday after hundreds of protesters stormed the U.S. Consulate in the Pakistani port city of Karachi, authorities said.

title: Continuous fuel distribution today and tomorrow:  Petroleum Tanker Owners
byline: Despite the ongoing war in the Middle East, the Sri Lanka Petroleum Private Tanker Owners’ Association has reassured the general public that there is sufficient fuel stock for the next 37 days in Sri Lanka, urging people not to panic unnecessarily.

title: No Sri Lankans in Israel harmed in Iran attacks: Ambassador
byline: No Sri Lankans have been known to be harmed in Israel following retaliatory attacks launched by Iran, the Ambassador of Sri Lanka to Israel, Nimal Bandara has confirmed to Ada Derana.

title: Senior Iranian official says no US warships can enter Persian Gulf
byline: No US warships would be allowed to enter the Persian Gulf Moshen Rezaei, a former top Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps commander and senior Iranian official, said Sunday.

title: Committee to be appointed to formulate national medical education policy – PM Harini
byline: Prime Minister Dr. Harini Amarasuriya has stated that a committee will be appointed to formulate a national policy on medical education, following requests by student unions emphasizing the need for such a policy.

title: Bruno Mars returns after nearly a decade with fourth solo album ‘The Romantic’
byline: Pop superstar Bruno Mars ended a nearly 10-year solo hiatus with the release of his fourth studio album, ‘The Romantic.’

title: SL Embassy in Saudi Arabia urges Sri Lankans to remain vigilant amid evolving security situation
byline: The Embassy of Sri Lanka in Saudi Arabia has urged Sri Lankans residing in Saudi Arabia to remain attentive and cautious in light of the evolving security situation in the Middle East.

title: Iran launches new wave of strikes against Israel, US bases in Middle East
byline: Iran has launched a new wave of missile and drone strikes targeting US military bases in the region and Israel.

title: All flights between Sri Lanka and Middle East temporarily suspended until 5 pm
byline: Due to the closure of airspace across Middle Eastern countries, all flights scheduled to depart from Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) to destinations in the Middle East have been temporarily suspended until 5.00 p.m. today (01).

title: Middle East Conflict: Foreign Ministry issues emergency contact numbers
byline: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has issued a set of emergency contact numbers for Sri Lankans in light of the escalating conflict in the Middle East.

title: Foreign-made pistol and ammunition recovered near cemetery in Katana
byline: A foreign-manufactured pistol along with four rounds of ammunition has been discovered while concealed in a shrubbery area on a by-road near the Mahayaya Waththa cemetery in Katana, police stated.

title: China urges immediate ceasefire after US, Israel strike Iran
byline: China’s ministry of foreign affairs has expressed concern over United States-Israeli strikes against Iran and called for an immediate ceasefire, urging all sides to avoid escalation and to resume dialogue and negotiation.

title: Oil tanker attacked near coast of Oman
byline: An oil tanker was attacked about five nautical miles off the coast of Masandam in Oman, the country’s maritime security center said Sunday.

title: Middle East Conflict causes worst travel disruptions in years
byline: Thousands of flights have been affected across the Middle East and beyond since the U.S. and Israeli strikes, according to data on FlightAware, a flight tracking platform.

title: Two Israelis killed, 120 injured in missile attack - IDF
byline: Two Israelis have been killed and over 120 injured in the two days since Operation Roaring Lion began, the Israel Defense Forces has confirmed.

title: National Drug Prevention Week commences today
byline: The “National Drug Prevention Week”, implemented in parallel with the “A Nation United” national campaign, is scheduled to commence today (01).

title: Homeowner overpowers gunman in attempted shooting
byline: An unidentified gunman who broke into a house, and attempted to shoot the homeowner has been overpowered by the homeowner, who attacked him with a chair and a sharp weapon.

title: Several senior Iranian figures killed in attack by US and Israel
byline: A number of senior figures in Iran are known to have been killed in Saturday’s strikes on Iran.

title: Israel targets “heart of Tehran” in new wave of strikes
byline: The Israeli Air Force has carried out a new wave of strikes on the Iranian capital Tehran, according to the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF).

title: Sri Lanka’s fuel supply chain not from current conflict zone - CPC Chairman
byline: There will be no delays in receiving fuel stocks ordered through April and May, the Ceylon Petroleum Corporation (CPC) stated.

title: Rosé makes history for K-Pop with BRIT Awards 2026 win
byline: Rosé winning Best International Song at the BRIT Awards 2026 marks a landmark moment for Korean musicians overseas as the BLACKPINK star is the first K-pop artist to take home a win at the U.K.’s biggest night for music. MORE.

title: CPC assures adequate fuel supply; urges motorists to avoid panic buying
byline: The Ceylon Petroleum Corporation (CPC) has confirmed that the country has adequate fuel stocks to last for more than a month.

title: Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian, Judiciary Chief and Guardian Council Jurist to temporarily lead Iran
byline: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, head of judiciary, and one jurist of the Guardian Council to temporarily assume duties of leadership in Iran after Supreme Leader Khamenei’s death, Iran media said.

title: Who is Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s Supreme Leader who ruled for 36 years?
byline: When targeted by nationwide protests early this year, Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei unleashed the bloodiest crackdown of his nearly four decades in power. But now a U.S. or Israeli military strike may have ended his rule.

title: Iran speaker brands US, Israel chiefs ‘filthy criminals,’ warns of retaliation
byline: Iran’s parliament speaker on Sunday called the leaders of the United States and Israel “filthy criminals” who will face “devastating blows” for their ongoing attacks on the Islamic Republic.

title: Iran’s Khamenei ‘will not be mourned,’ says Australian PM Albanese
byline: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says that Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei “will not be mourned,” as state media in the Islamic Republic confirms he has been killed.

title: 115 flights between Sri Lanka and Middle East cancelled; Cancellations continue today
byline: All flights from Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) to the Middle East scheduled for today (01 March) have also been cancelled due to the ongoing conflict in the region.

title: Funds allocated for provincial school infrastructure; PM urges submission of development requests
byline: Prime Minister Harini Amarasuriya stated that the necessary financial allocations for the development of infrastructure in provincial schools have been provided to all provinces.

title: Iran FM: Oman events not intentional; military units now independent and isolated, acting on prior directives
byline: Iran’s Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi has said that recent incidents in Oman were not deliberate, stressing that Tehran did not intend to widen the conflict across the Gulf.

title: Iran ‘will not negotiate with US’: Top Iranian official
byline: Secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council Ali Larijani denied Monday claims that Tehran had sought to restart negotiations with Washington, declaring that Iran will not engage in talks with the US.

title: Sri Lanka grants 2-week visa extension for foreigners stranded due to flight restrictions
byline: Sri Lanka’s Cabinet Spokesperson and Minister Dr. Nalinda Jayatissa stated that, due to flight restrictions caused by the Middle East conflict, the visa validity period for foreigners stranded in the country will be extended by two weeks.

title: SriLankan Airlines announcement on cancelled flights
byline: SriLankan Airlines has announced that due to the continued closure of airspace in certain parts of the Middle East, the SriLankan Airlines flights scheduled to operate today (02) have been cancelled.

title: As Israel bombs Tehran, ‘series of blasts’ over Jerusalem
byline: A series of loud blasts were heard above Jerusalem following sirens on Sunday evening, AFP journalists reported, after the Israeli military said it had detected missiles launched from Iran.

title: Suspected drone strike hits British air base in Cyprus
byline: A drone strike hit Britain’s Royal Air Force base of Akrotiri in Cyprus overnight, causing limited damage and no casualties, the island’s president and Britain’s defence ministry said on Monday.

title: Police urge students and parents to uphold discipline at inter-school cricket tournaments
byline: Sri Lanka Police have issued an advisory for students, parents, and alumni attending annual inter-school cricket tournaments, emphasizing the importance of discipline, safety, and respect for public order.

title: 21-year-old arrested at BIA with cigarettes worth over Rs. 5 million
byline: A 21-year-old businessman has been arrested at the Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) while attempting to smuggle a large consignment of foreign-manufactured cigarettes into the country.

title: Vampire tale ‘Sinners’ wins top Actor Award and sets up Oscars battle
byline: Supernatural thriller ‘‘Sinners’‘ claimed the Actor Award for best movie cast on Sunday, a victory that sets up a face-off with ‘‘One Battle After Another’‘ for the prestigious Best Picture prize at the upcoming Oscars.

title: Oman-based airline lands at BIA
byline: A flight operated by Oman-based SalamAir landed in Sri Lanka early this morning (02), the Director General of the Civil Aviation Authority of Sri Lanka, Captain Daminda Rambukwella stated.

title: Millions in Iran without internet for more than 48 hours
byline: Iran’s internet blackout has now lasted for more than 48 hours, according to cybersecurity watchdog NetBlocks, as the war enters its third day.

title: Middle East Conflict: No Sri Lankans injured in Iran airstrikes in UAE
byline: No injuries or casualties involving Sri Lankans in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) have been reported thus far, the Ambassador of Sri Lanka to the United Arab Emirates, Arusha Cooray has stated.

title: Cabinet spokesman hints at possible extension to state of public emergency
byline: The government is considering extending the nationwide state of public emergency, Cabinet Spokesman Minister Dr. Nalinda Jayatissa stated today.

title: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike
byline: Saudi Arabia’s state oil giant Aramco shut its Ras Tanura refinery following a drone strike, an industry source said on Monday, after Tehran launched strikes across the region in response to the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran.

title: Motorcycle catches fire at Jaffna filling station
byline: A motorcycle caught fire at a filling station near the Maruthanarmadam Junction in Jaffna, while being refueled.

title: QR system for fuel unnecessary, CPC Chairman asserts
byline: The QR system should only be used to limit fuel distribution in the event of a fuel shortage in the country, the Chairman of the Ceylon Petroleum Corporation (CPC), D.J. Rajakaruna stated.

title: At least 31 killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon, as conflict widens
byline: Israeli strikes on Lebanon have killed at least 31 people, authorities said, following rocket fire from Tehran-backed militant group Hezbollah after the killing of Iran’s supreme leader.

title: Kuwait says several US warplanes have crashed in the country today
byline: Kuwait’s Ministry of Defense said “several United States military aircraft crashed” on Monday and that “all crews survived.”

title: Over 550 killed in Iran by US-Israeli strikes on 131 cities, Red Crescent says
byline: At least 555 people have been killed in Iran since joint US-Israeli strikes began on Saturday, according to the Red Crescent Society.

title: State of Public Emergency in Sri Lanka extended
byline: The State of Public Emergency in Sri Lanka has been extended.

title: Oil prices jump and shares fall as conflict escalates
byline: Global oil prices have risen as Iran continues to launch strikes across the Middle East in response to ongoing attacks by the US and Israel.

title: Chinese FM calls for immediate stop of military operations in Middle East
byline: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Monday that the urgent priority concerning the current situation in the Middle East is to immediately halt military operations and prevent the conflict from further spilling over.

title: Wife of late supreme leader has been killed: Iran state TV
byline: Iran state TV Channel Two has confirmed that Mansoureh Khojaste Bagherzadeh, wife of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, has also been killed “at home”.

title: “Stop the war and commence peace talks promptly”: Sajith
byline: Prevailing aggression and conflict in the Middle East is a significant threat to global peace and security, Leader of the Opposition Sajith Premadasa stated.

title: Externally forced regime change unacceptable: Ex-President Ranil
byline: The concept of externally forced regime change is unacceptable, according to former President Ranil Wickremesinghe.

title: Ambassador assures safety of Sri Lankans in Kuwait amid regional tensions
byline: The Sri Lankan Ambassador to Kuwait, Lakshitha Ratnayake has reassured that Sri Lankans residing in Kuwait are being closely monitored and are safe following recent regional tensions.

title: Efforts underway to repatriate Sri Lankans stranded at various airports in Middle East: Minister
byline: All flights to and from the Middle East have been suspended until midnight (02) due to the ongoing tensions in the region, the Minister of Ports and Civil Aviation, Anura Karunathilaka has confirmed.

title: Immigration Dept. issues notice on visa extension for stranded foreign nationals
byline: Visa extensions will be granted for 14 days for foreign nationals who are unable to depart Sri Lanka due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, the Department of Immigration and Emigration has announced.

title: Fourth US service member dies, military says
byline: A fourth U.S. service member has died of injuries sustained in the U.S. operation in Iran, the Central Command said.

title: Three US fighter jets went down in Kuwait due to friendly fire incident: US Central Command
byline: Three US fighter jets went down “due to an apparent friendly fire incident,” US Central Command has announced.

title: Limited number of flights to resume from Dubai this evening
byline: Dubai Airports say “a limited resumption” of flights will begin this evening, with a “small number of flights” from Dubai International (DXB) and Dubai World Central – Al Maktoum International (DWC).

title: UN nuclear watchdog says no sign US-Israeli attacks have hit Iran’s nuclear facilities
byline: The UN nuclear watchdog said there was no indication that Israeli and US strikes on Iran have hit any nuclear facilities.

title: We didn’t start this war but we are finishing it: US Defense Secretary
byline: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said today that the US strikes on Iran is not meant to be “regime change,” while he acknowledged that the leadership in the country has changed.

title: Gulf countries and US condemn Iran’s missile and drone attacks
byline: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, the UAE, and the United States have issued a joint statement strongly condemning the indiscriminate missile and drone attacks carried out by the Islamic Republic of Iran against sovereign territories in the region, according to the Qatar News Agency (QNA).

title: Ex-President Mahinda urges swift end to Middle East conflict
byline: The military conflict in the Middle East must be brought to an end as soon as possible, former President Mahinda Rajapaksa has stated.

title: UAE intercepted 9 ballistic missiles, 6 cruise missiles and 148 drones today
byline: Nine ballistic missiles, six cruise missiles and 148 drones were successfully intercepted by the United Arab Emirates’ (UAE) air defences, according to its Ministry of Defence (MoD).
